steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The first clause gives Nobel's criterion for the Literature prize. The next clause gives a 1909 example involving Lagerlof. Since both parts are complete independent clauses, they need punctuation that can join closely related complete thoughts.

steps.title: Use a semicolon after the quote
steps.content: A adds an unnecessary conjunction after the quoted phrase. C leaves the two clauses without punctuation. D uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. A semicolon correctly links the criterion to the example. The answer is B.
